checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?
checks.guidance: Cross-check the result against explicit user notes, critique corrections, and watch items. Flag anything the implementation ignored, contradicted, or only partially covered.

findings.detail: Checked the current branch state against the milestone deliverables and confirmed that `zustand` is already committed in [package.json](/workspace/reigh-app/package.json:71) and [package-lock.json](/workspace/reigh-app/package-lock.json:37), while the shared factory and baseline tests already exist in [createEntityStore.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts:1) and [createEntityStore.test.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.test.ts:1). That means the plan is correctly about reshaping existing foundation code rather than performing a first-time install or introducing a parallel store abstraction.

Checked the repository docs again and the brief still names `docs/state_management_refactor_plan.md`, but this checkout only has [docs/state_management_refactor_baselines.md](/workspace/reigh-app/docs/state_management_refactor_baselines.md:1) plus the settings docs under [docs/structure_detail/settings_system.md](/workspace/reigh-app/docs/structure_detail/settings_system.md:1). The revised plan now documents that mismatch, but milestone traceability is still prompt-driven rather than repo-driven, so the original verifiability gap remains open.

prior_findings.detail: Checked the repository against the milestone brief and found that both prerequisite foundation pieces already exist in this branch: `zustand` is already listed in [package.json](/workspace/reigh-app/package.json:62) and the shared factory plus baseline tests already live in [createEntityStore.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts:1) and [createEntityStore.test.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.test.ts:1). The revision correctly pivots M1 toward finalizing and reshaping that existing foundation instead of adding a second copy.

Checked the repo for the milestone source document named in the brief and only found [docs/structure_detail/settings_system.md](/workspace/reigh-app/docs/structure_detail/settings_system.md:1); the referenced `docs/state_management_refactor_plan.md` is not present anywhere under `/workspace/reigh-app`. That means the plan can only be validated against the prompt text, not against a repository document the executor can re-open, which is still a verifiability gap for an implementation milestone tied to a named doc.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?
checks.guidance: Look for logic errors, invalid assumptions, broken invariants, schema mismatches, or behavior that would fail at runtime. When the fix adds a conditional branch, check whether it handles all relevant cases — not just the one reported in the issue.

findings.detail: Re-checked the nullish-`projectId` edge against the actual hook signature in [useJoinClipsSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsSettings.ts:9), the current null/undefined tests in [useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/__tests__/useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts:62), and the string-only factory API in [createEntityStore.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts:31). The new sentinel-entity bridge is a technically coherent way to keep `useEntity(entityId: string)` unconditional while still masking disabled callers behind the current idle/default/no-op contract, so the earlier hook-order/runtime edge looks resolved.

Checked the existing `useAutoSaveSettings` contract in [useAutoSaveSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/settings/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings.ts:283), [useAutoSaveSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/settings/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings.ts:442), and [useAutoSaveSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/settings/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings.ts:459). The revised plan now explicitly preserves the two behavior-sensitive pieces that mattered most in this repo: `saveImmediate(dataToSave?)` remains an override-capable save surface, and `initializeFrom(...)` stays a no-op for the React Query-backed mode instead of being remapped to local store seeding.

prior_findings.detail: Re-checked the two previously-blocking contract points against [useAutoSaveSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/settings/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings.ts:431) and [useAutoSaveSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/settings/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings.ts:459). The revision now keeps `initializeFrom` as a React Query-mode no-op and explicitly moves the clean baseline on `reset(newDefaults?)`, so it resolves the earlier contract drift around no-op initialization and clean-after-reset behavior.

Checked the adapter edge cases against the actual hook signature in [useJoinClipsSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsSettings.ts:9), its null/undefined tests in [useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/__tests__/useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts:68), and the string-only factory API in [createEntityStore.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts:31). The plan says nullish `projectId` must still produce idle/defaults/no-op behavior, but it never explains how that is implemented without either calling `useEntity` conditionally or inventing a sentinel entity ID, so one real runtime edge remains underspecified for the migrated adapter.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?
checks.guidance: Look at how the changed function is used across the codebase. Does the fix only address one caller's scenario while others remain broken? Flag missing required work or out-of-scope edits. A minimal patch is often right, but check whether the underlying problem is bigger than what the issue describes.

findings.detail: Checked the real Join Clips settings surface in [defaults.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/lib/joinClips/defaults.ts:12) and the write paths in [useClipManager.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useClipManager.ts:175) and [JoinClipsPage.tsx](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx:95). In this repo `useJoinClipsSettings` is no longer a narrow five-field adapter: it carries the full VACE-derived settings object plus persisted `clips`, `transitionPrompts`, `loras`, and loop/FPS toggles, so porting it is a broader first migration than the milestone summary suggests.

Searched the production call sites and related helpers in [JoinClipsPage.tsx](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx:248), [useClipManager.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useClipManager.ts:32), and [useJoinClipsPageHelpers.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/pages/hooks/useJoinClipsPageHelpers.ts:15). Even with that broader settings payload, the migration target is still intentionally contained to one production hook and its local consumers, so the plan does not accidentally spill into other `useAutoSaveSettings` adapters in this milestone.

prior_findings.detail: Searched the Join Clips surface in [JoinClipsPage.tsx](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx:248), [useClipManager.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useClipManager.ts:32), and [useJoinClipsPageHelpers.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/pages/hooks/useJoinClipsPageHelpers.ts:13). The migration target is still intentionally narrow: there is one production consumer hook and a small set of callers that read its returned object, so limiting M1 to `useJoinClipsSettings` remains consistent with the actual repo.

Re-checked the earlier double-read concern against the revised steps and the existing merged-settings path in [useToolSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/hooks/settings/useToolSettings.ts:163). The plan now explicitly keeps `useToolSettings` as the only authoritative merged-read source for Join Clips and demotes the store `load()` path to neutral entity bootstrap, which contains the first consumer to one merged-settings runtime instead of two.

checks.question: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?
checks.guidance: Search for all instances of the symbol/pattern being changed. Also ask: does this feature require setup, registration, or integration code beyond the core logic? Missing glue code causes test failures even when the core fix is correct.

findings.detail: Checked the actual glue points that have to move together: the shared store in [createEntityStore.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts:1), its tests in [createEntityStore.test.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.test.ts:1), the Join Clips wrapper in [useJoinClipsSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsSettings.ts:1), the low-level cache/update boundary in [useToolSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/hooks/settings/useToolSettings.ts:1), and the lifecycle flush behavior currently supplied by [useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/settings/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts:66). The revised plan names those real integration points, so it is no longer treating this as a one-file hook rewrite.

Checked the current Join Clips test surface and found that [useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/__tests__/useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts:1) is still coupled to the old `useAutoSaveSettings` delegation detail, while page and helper tests consume the hook only through its public return shape. Rewriting the hook test around behavior rather than delegation is therefore the necessary supporting test change, and I did not find another production adopter of `createEntityStore` that would require parallel migration work in M1.

prior_findings.detail: Checked the supporting infrastructure around the named edit points: the existing shared store file and tests are [createEntityStore.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts:1) and [createEntityStore.test.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.test.ts:1); the Join Clips adapter is [useJoinClipsSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsSettings.ts:1); the persistence/cache boundary is [useToolSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/hooks/settings/useToolSettings.ts:1); and the flush/query behavior that the adapter is replacing lives in [useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/settings/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts:66). The revised plan now touches the real glue points instead of pretending this is just a one-file hook swap.

Searched for current `createEntityStore` consumers with `rg -n "createEntityStore\(" src` and found no production call sites beyond the factory definition itself. That means the plan does not miss any existing adopter that would also need migration work in this milestone; the only required integration surface is the new Join Clips adapter plus its tests.

checks.question: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?
checks.guidance: Grep for call sites. For each caller, check what values it passes — especially edge cases like None, zero, empty, or composite inputs. Then ask: should this change be here, or in a caller, callee, or new method?

findings.detail: Checked all current callers of `useJoinClipsSettings(...)` and found one production site in [JoinClipsPage.tsx](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx:248) plus the hook test file. The production caller passes `selectedProjectId`, which is `string | null`, and computes `settingsLoaded` from `status !== 'idle' && status !== 'loading'` before the page later short-circuits on a missing project, so preserving the nullish idle-state contract is not optional in this codebase.

Checked downstream usage in [useClipManager.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useClipManager.ts:120), [JoinClipsPage.tsx](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx:252), and [useJoinClipsGenerate.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsGenerate.ts:25). These callers consume the hook through its existing adapter surface rather than a raw entity-store view, so the plan is right to keep the migrated hook returning the current Join Clips contract instead of exposing `createEntityStore` directly.

prior_findings.detail: Checked all current `useJoinClipsSettings(...)` call sites in [JoinClipsPage.tsx](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx:248) and its tests in [useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/__tests__/useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts:52). Callers only pass either a selected project ID string or a nullish value, and downstream consumers primarily read `settings`, `status`, `updateField`, and `updateFields`, so preserving the public adapter surface instead of exposing the raw store view is still the right direction.

Checked the caller-sensitive contract around nullish arguments against [useJoinClipsSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsSettings.ts:9) and the existing tests for `null` and `undefined` in [useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/__tests__/useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts:68). The revision still needs an explicit adapter rule for these callers because the factory API only accepts `string` entity IDs; without that bridge, the executor could accidentally allocate a fake shared entity or violate the Rules of Hooks while trying to preserve the current idle/no-op behavior.

flags.concern: Plan source verifiability: the repository still does not contain the referenced `docs/state_management_refactor_plan.md`, so milestone-to-repo traceability remains dependent on the prompt rather than a repo document the executor can inspect.
flags.evidence: Only /workspace/reigh-app/docs/state_management_refactor_baselines.md and the structure_detail docs are present; no file named docs/state_management_refactor_plan.md exists anywhere under /workspace/reigh-app.

flags.concern: Join-clips settings scope: the "smallest consumer, 5 fields" framing no longer matches the repository, because `useJoinClipsSettings` now carries VACE defaults plus persisted clip/prompt/LoRA arrays and related toggles.
flags.evidence: src/shared/lib/joinClips/defaults.ts spreads VACE_GENERATION_DEFAULTS and includes clips/transitionPrompts/loras/loopFirstClip/useInputVideoFps, and src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useClipManager.ts persists clips and transitionPrompts through joinSettings.updateFields(...).
